   "source": [
    "# Lock-in Detection: Recovering Weak AM/FM Structure\n",
    "\n",
    "[![Open In Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/assets/colab-badge.svg)](https://colab.research.google.com/github/tatsuki-washimi/gwexpy/blob/main/docs/web/en/user_guide/tutorials/case_lockin_detection.ipynb)\n"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "e1865f7a",
   "metadata": {
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 0.006107,
     "end_time": "2026-04-27T16:14:27.304039+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-04-27T16:14:27.297932+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": [
     "lang-en"
    ]
   },
   "source": [
    "Lock-in detection is the right tool when the physically interesting information lives as a **slow modulation** on a much faster carrier. The goal is not to measure the carrier itself, but to move the modulation into baseband where low-frequency trend tools can recover it.\n",
    "\n",
    "This public version is distilled from the legacy heterodyne notebook and focuses on the essential AM/FM recovery workflow."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 2,
   "id": "9a3de89e",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-04-27T16:14:27.529341Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-04-27T16:14:27.529123Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-04-27T16:14:34.784097Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-04-27T16:14:34.783669Z"
    },
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 7.466726,
     "end_time": "2026-04-27T16:14:34.787368+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-04-27T16:14:27.320642+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": []
   },
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "import matplotlib.pyplot as plt\n",
    "import numpy as np\n",
    "from scipy import signal\n",
    "\n",
    "from gwexpy import TimeSeries\n",
    "\n",
    "fs = 1024.0\n",
    "duration = 40.0\n",
    "t = np.arange(0, duration, 1 / fs)\n",
    "np.random.seed(42)\n",
    "\n",
    "# The carrier is the strong narrowband tone that transports the information we actually care about.\n",
    "f_carrier = 100.0\n",
    "\n",
    "# Slow AM and PM terms encode the physical drift to be recovered after demodulation.\n",
    "amp_mod = 1.0 + 0.5 * np.sin(2 * np.pi * 0.2 * t)\n",
    "phase_mod = 2.0 * np.sin(2 * np.pi * 0.05 * t)\n",
    "\n",
    "clean_signal = amp_mod * np.cos(2 * np.pi * f_carrier * t + phase_mod)\n",
    "noise = np.random.normal(0, 0.8, len(t))\n",
    "ts = TimeSeries(clean_signal + noise, sample_rate=fs, name=\"Modulated Signal\", unit=\"V\")\n"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "9896b440",
   "metadata": {
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 0.00168,
     "end_time": "2026-04-27T16:14:34.792707+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-04-27T16:14:34.791027+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": [
     "lang-en"
    ]
   },
   "source": [
    "## 1. Recover amplitude and phase with `lock_in`\n",
    "\n",
    "The reference frequency tells the algorithm which coherent part of the waveform to keep. Broadband noise averages away, while the slow AM/FM structure survives."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 3,
   "id": "8c6a1930",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-04-27T16:14:34.810309Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-04-27T16:14:34.809919Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-04-27T16:14:35.670204Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-04-27T16:14:35.669656Z"
    },
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 0.869009,
     "end_time": "2026-04-27T16:14:35.670951+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-04-27T16:14:34.801942+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": []
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure size 1200x800 with 2 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "# stride sets the time resolution of the recovered baseband trend: shorter stride follows faster modulation but averages less noise.\n",
    "amp, phase = ts.lock_in(f0=f_carrier, stride=0.1, output=\"amp_phase\", deg=False)\n",
    "\n",
    "fig, (ax1, ax2) = plt.subplots(2, 1, figsize=(12, 8), sharex=True)\n",
    "ax1.plot(amp, label=\"Extracted Amplitude\", color=\"red\")\n",
    "ax1.plot(t, amp_mod, label=\"True Amplitude\", color=\"black\", ls=\"--\", alpha=0.7)\n",
    "ax1.set_ylabel(\"Amplitude [V]\")\n",
    "ax1.legend()\n",
    "\n",
    "ax2.plot(phase, label=\"Extracted Phase\", color=\"blue\")\n",
    "ax2.plot(t, phase_mod, label=\"True Phase\", color=\"black\", ls=\"--\", alpha=0.7)\n",
    "ax2.set_ylabel(\"Phase [rad]\")\n",
    "ax2.legend()\n",
    "plt.xlabel(\"Time [s]\")\n",
    "plt.show()\n"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "4650d1cb",
   "metadata": {
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 0.010023,
     "end_time": "2026-04-27T16:14:35.701349+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-04-27T16:14:35.691326+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": [
     "lang-en"
    ]
   },
   "source": [
    "## 2. Manual demodulation shows the same physics\n",
    "\n",
    "Mixing with cosine/sine creates a DC term plus an image at `2*f_c`. The low-pass filter rejects that high-frequency image so only the slow envelope and phase drift remain."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 4,
   "id": "ed160465",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-04-27T16:14:35.799910Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-04-27T16:14:35.799515Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-04-27T16:14:36.123160Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-04-27T16:14:36.119820Z"
    },
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 0.3355,
     "end_time": "2026-04-27T16:14:36.125395+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-04-27T16:14:35.789895+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": []
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure size 1200x400 with 1 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "ref_cos = np.cos(2 * np.pi * f_carrier * t)\n",
    "ref_sin = np.sin(2 * np.pi * f_carrier * t)\n",
    "\n",
    "# Multiply by the reference to shift the carrier to baseband and create a mirrored image at 2*f_c.\n",
    "mixed_i = (ts * ref_cos) * 2.0\n",
    "mixed_q = (ts * (-ref_sin)) * 2.0\n",
    "\n",
    "# Low-pass after mixing removes the 2*f_c image term so the slow modulation remains.\n",
    "b, a = signal.butter(4, 2.0, fs=fs, btype=\"low\")\n",
    "i_filtered = TimeSeries(signal.filtfilt(b, a, mixed_i.value), sample_rate=fs)\n",
    "q_filtered = TimeSeries(signal.filtfilt(b, a, mixed_q.value), sample_rate=fs)\n",
    "\n",
    "amp_manual = np.sqrt(i_filtered**2 + q_filtered**2)\n",
    "phase_manual = np.arctan2(q_filtered.value, i_filtered.value)\n",
    "\n",
    "plt.figure(figsize=(12, 4))\n",
    "plt.plot(amp_manual, label=\"Manual Amplitude\")\n",
    "plt.plot(amp, label=\"lock_in()\", alpha=0.7)\n",
    "plt.legend()\n",
    "plt.show()\n"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "89cc0221",
   "metadata": {
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 0.077993,
     "end_time": "2026-04-27T16:14:36.211107+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-04-27T16:14:36.133114+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": [
     "lang-en"
    ]
   },
   "source": [
    "## 3. Demodulation moves the information into baseband\n",
    "\n",
    "After demodulation, the interesting structure is centered near zero frequency. That is why lock-in detection turns a high-frequency carrier problem into a low-frequency trend-analysis problem."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 5,
   "id": "4bb4938a",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-04-27T16:14:36.669345Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-04-27T16:14:36.669087Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-04-27T16:14:38.382240Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-04-27T16:14:38.381658Z"
    },
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 1.769218,
     "end_time": "2026-04-27T16:14:38.385313+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-04-27T16:14:36.616095+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": []
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure size 1000x600 with 1 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "asd_raw = ts.asd(fftlength=4)\n",
    "complex_signal = ts.lock_in(f0=f_carrier, stride=1 / fs, output=\"complex\")\n",
    "asd_demod = complex_signal.asd(fftlength=4)\n",
    "\n",
    "plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))\n",
    "plt.loglog(asd_raw.frequencies, asd_raw.value, label=\"Original Signal\")\n",
    "plt.loglog(asd_demod.frequencies, asd_demod.value, label=\"Demodulated Signal\")\n",
    "plt.axvline(f_carrier, color=\"red\", ls=\"--\", label=\"Carrier\")\n",
    "plt.legend()\n",
    "plt.grid(True, which=\"both\")\n",
    "plt.show()\n"
   ]
  }
 ],
